description_long: This sconce features a vertically elongated, ornate structure crafted from what appears to be bronze or brass, with a patinated, aged finish. The topmost section is adorned with a sculpted bird, likely an eagle, with outstretched wings and a keyhole-shaped loop above its head, serving as a decorative finial. Below the bird, the body of the sconce is composed of a series of turned, bulbous segments that taper and expand, giving it a classical, almost ceremonial appearance. The lower portion culminates in a spherical form with a pointed tip, adding to its visual weight and balance. The surface is matte with subtle textural variations, suggesting hand-finishing or casting marks. This object is likely intended for wall mounting and would serve as a decorative and functional lighting fixture in a traditional or historic urban setting.
description: An ornate, bronze or brass sconce with a sculpted bird finial, turned segments, and a spherical base, finished in a matte patina.
description_view_0: The sconce is seen at an angle, highlighting the bird finial and the layered, turned segments, with the spherical base partially visible.
description_view_1: A direct frontal view reveals the full symmetry of the bird finial and the central axis of the sconce, with all major segments clearly aligned.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the profile of the bird and the depth of the turned segments, with the spherical base receding.
description_view_3: The opposite angle to view 0, showing the bird finial from the other side and the curvature of the sconce's body.
attribute_fixture_design: Vintage and classical, with ornate, ceremonial motifs and a sculpted bird finial, reminiscent of historic or antique European sconces.
